scope: Authentication plus event creation/management local red-team pass

findings.title: Mutable email is used as an admin credential
findings.rootCause: Admin status trusts users.email, while users can update email without ownership proof or uniqueness.
findings.fix: Remove email-string admin checks. Store immutable admin assignments server-side and require verified email-change workflow.

controls.title: Normal user becomes admin by setting email to ADMIN_EMAIL
controls.expected: Users must not be able to alter their own admin trust attribute.

controls.title: Login by spoofed admin email returns attacker-controlled admin session
controls.expected: Email aliases must not let an attacker create an admin-recognized principal.

findings.title: Developer-email registration bypass grants admin-capable role
findings.rootCause: Public registration grants BRAHMACHARI when username matches DEVELOPER_EMAILS, without proving email ownership.
findings.fix: Disable the bypass outside local dev and provision developer roles through an authenticated admin flow.

controls.title: Invite-only registration still allows listed developer email without invite
controls.expected: Invite-only mode should block public role-bearing registration without invite and email proof.

controls.title: Developer self-registration receives admin-capable verificationLevel
controls.expected: Self-registered users should not receive admin-capable role solely from email string.

controls.title: Developer self-registration can call admin endpoint
controls.expected: New public signup should not be able to read admin data.

findings.title: Inactive and suspended users remain fully authenticated
findings.affected: POST /api/auth/authenticate, GET /api/auth/verify, POST /api/auth/refresh, event write routes
findings.rootCause: authMiddleware and login/refresh flows do not reject is_active=0 or suspended accounts.
findings.fix: Enforce active and non-suspended state on login, refresh, and every authenticated request.

controls.title: Inactive user token verifies successfully
controls.expected: Inactive accounts should be rejected by auth middleware.

controls.title: Inactive user can refresh session
controls.expected: Inactive accounts should not receive fresh tokens.

controls.title: Inactive sevak can create event
controls.expected: Inactive coordinator account should not write events.

controls.title: Suspended user token verifies successfully
controls.expected: Suspended accounts should be rejected by auth middleware or high-risk writes.

controls.title: Suspended user can refresh session
controls.expected: Suspended accounts should not receive fresh tokens.

controls.title: Suspended sevak can create event
controls.expected: Suspended coordinator account should not write events.

findings.title: Auth and event abuse throttles trust spoofable IP headers
findings.affected: rateLimit middleware on auth and event RSVP routes
findings.cwe: CWE-348 Use of Less Trusted Source
findings.rootCause: The rate limiter uses request-supplied CF-Connecting-IP and X-Forwarded-For headers.
findings.fix: Use trusted platform request metadata only and add per-account/per-event throttles.

controls.title: Login limiter bypassed with varied forwarding headers
controls.expected: Limiter should key on trusted client identity and continue blocking with spoofed forwarding headers.

controls.title: Register limiter bypassed with varied forwarding headers
controls.expected: Limiter should key on trusted client identity and continue blocking with spoofed forwarding headers.

controls.title: Password reset request limiter bypassed with varied forwarding headers
controls.expected: Limiter should key on trusted client identity and continue blocking with spoofed forwarding headers.

controls.title: Password reset verify limiter bypassed with varied forwarding headers
controls.expected: Limiter should key on trusted client identity and continue blocking with spoofed forwarding headers.

controls.title: Invite-code validation limiter bypassed with varied forwarding headers
controls.expected: Limiter should key on trusted client identity and continue blocking with spoofed forwarding headers.

controls.title: Email verification send limiter bypassed with varied forwarding headers
controls.expected: Limiter should key on trusted client identity and continue blocking with spoofed forwarding headers.

findings.title: Malformed JSON exposes internal parser errors across auth/event routes
findings.affected: Global error handler plus routes using c.req.json without local catch
findings.cwe: CWE-209 Information Exposure Through Error Message
findings.rootCause: app.onError returns exception messages to the client.
findings.fix: Return generic error bodies externally and keep detailed parser errors in logs only.

controls.title: POST /auth/register leaks parser/internal error on malformed JSON
controls.expected: Malformed JSON should return a generic 400 without internals.

controls.title: POST /auth/authenticate leaks parser/internal error on malformed JSON
controls.expected: Malformed JSON should return a generic 400 without internals.

controls.title: PUT /auth/update-profile leaks parser/internal error on malformed JSON
controls.expected: Malformed JSON should return a generic 400 without internals.

controls.title: POST /addEvent leaks parser/internal error on malformed JSON
controls.expected: Malformed JSON should return a generic 400 without internals.

controls.title: POST /updateEvent leaks parser/internal error on malformed JSON
controls.expected: Malformed JSON should return a generic 400 without internals.

controls.title: POST /removeEvent leaks parser/internal error on malformed JSON
controls.expected: Malformed JSON should return a generic 400 without internals.

controls.title: POST /attendEvent leaks parser/internal error on malformed JSON
controls.expected: Malformed JSON should return a generic 400 without internals.

controls.title: POST /unattendEvent leaks parser/internal error on malformed JSON
controls.expected: Malformed JSON should return a generic 400 without internals.

controls.title: PUT /admin/events/:id leaks parser/internal error on malformed JSON
controls.expected: Malformed JSON should return a generic 400 without internals.

controls.title: POST /admin/users/:id/verify leaks parser/internal error on malformed JSON
controls.expected: Malformed JSON should return a generic 400 without internals.

findings.title: Profile mutation lets users rewrite security-sensitive identity fields
findings.cwe: CWE-639 Authorization Bypass Through User-Controlled Key
findings.rootCause: Self-service profile routes write email, center_id, and profile trust fields directly.
findings.fix: Split profile display fields from trust fields; require verified workflows or admin approval for email and center membership.

controls.title: Duplicate email can be assigned without verification
controls.expected: Email changes should require ownership proof and uniqueness.

controls.title: Invalid email string can be assigned to account
controls.expected: Email fields should be syntactically valid and verified.

controls.title: Unverified user can self-assign center through update-profile
controls.expected: Center membership should be server-controlled.

controls.title: Unverified user can self-assign center through complete-onboarding
controls.expected: Onboarding should not establish trusted membership without approval.

controls.title: Profile route stores oversized display fields and javascript image URL
controls.expected: Profile fields should use length limits and URL scheme allowlists.

findings.title: Session lifecycle is stateless and logout does not revoke tokens
findings.rootCause: Logout is a no-op and refresh tokens can be replayed until expiry or password rotation.
findings.fix: Store refresh-token identifiers, rotate on use, revoke on logout, and rate-limit refresh attempts.

controls.title: Logout/deauthenticate does not invalidate access token
controls.expected: Logout should revoke the current session.

controls.title: Same refresh token can be replayed multiple times
controls.expected: Refresh token rotation should invalidate the previous token on use.

controls.title: Refresh endpoint has no visible rate limit
controls.expected: Refresh attempts should be throttled.

findings.title: Unauthenticated account and invite state can be enumerated
findings.rootCause: Public endpoints return exact existence and invite-state differences.
findings.fix: Remove user existence endpoint and normalize invite validation responses where possible.

controls.title: Public userExistence distinguishes valid accounts from missing accounts
controls.expected: Public auth flow should not reveal account existence.

controls.title: Invite validation reveals inactive/expired/exhausted/not-found states
controls.expected: Invite validation should avoid unnecessarily precise state disclosure.

body.error: This invite link has been deactivated
This invite link has reached its maximum number of uses

findings.title: Email verification and password reset can be spammed per account
findings.cwe: CWE-770 Allocation of Resources Without Limits
findings.rootCause: Flows are throttled only by spoofable IP, not by account/email, and create multiple server-side tokens.
findings.fix: Add per-account cooldowns, invalidate or reuse email-verification tokens, and alert on repeated reset requests.

controls.title: Password reset request spam creates repeated reset-code rows for one account
controls.expected: Per-account cooldown should prevent repeated reset issuance.

controls.title: Email verification resend creates multiple simultaneously valid tokens
controls.expected: Resend should reuse or invalidate prior tokens and enforce per-account cooldown.

findings.title: Event creation is role-gated but not center-scoped
findings.rootCause: A sevak can create official events for any center without proving local coordinator scope.
findings.fix: Scope event creation to centers the coordinator manages, or require admin approval for cross-center events.

controls.title: Sevak for center A can create official event under center B
controls.expected: Coordinator should only create events for managed centers.

controls.title: Sevak can create official event under unverified center
controls.expected: Unverified centers should not host official events without admin approval.

findings.title: Event creation accepts unsafe or nonsensical field values
findings.rootCause: Create validation checks only a subset of fields and treats URL fields as length-bounded strings.
findings.fix: Use a strict shared event schema with URL scheme allowlists, date validation, category enum checks, and bounded optional fields.

findings.title: Event owner update path bypasses create-time validation
findings.rootCause: updateEvent writes fields directly, parses coordinates loosely, and validates almost none of the create constraints.
findings.fix: Route all event mutations through one strict schema and validate ownership scope before applying changes.

findings.title: Admin event update path has a separate validation bypass
findings.rootCause: Admin update duplicates event mutation logic and omits URL/date/length/category validation.
findings.fix: Share the same event schema for admin and owner updates; make admin override explicit and audited.

findings.title: Legacy or downgraded creators retain event-management power
findings.rootCause: Event management checks created_by/null-owner state, not current coordinator authorization.
findings.fix: Require current coordinator/admin authority for edit/delete/roster, and backfill or admin-lock null-owner events.

controls.title: Normal member can edit null-owner legacy event
controls.expected: Null-owner legacy events should be admin-locked or backfilled.

event.title: AE null owner edited by member

controls.title: Downgraded creator can still edit event
controls.expected: Current coordinator authorization should be required for edits.

controls.title: Downgraded creator can still view guest roster PII
controls.expected: Roster PII should require current coordinator/admin authority.

controls.title: Downgraded creator can still delete event
controls.expected: Delete should require current coordinator/admin authority.

findings.title: Event RSVP endpoints bypass frontend-only signup constraints
findings.rootCause: RSVP APIs ignore signup_url and allow_janata_signup, so hidden/alternate signup controls are only enforced in UI.
findings.fix: Enforce per-event signup policy server-side for both authenticated and guest RSVP routes.

controls.title: Authenticated RSVP succeeds for external-only event with allowJanataSignup=false
controls.expected: Server should reject native RSVP when event policy says external signup only.

controls.title: Guest RSVP succeeds for external-only event with allowJanataSignup=false
controls.expected: Guest API should enforce the same signup policy as the UI.

findings.title: Guest RSVP accepts unverified third-party identity claims
findings.rootCause: Guest RSVP stores arbitrary email/name as attendee-like data without confirming mailbox control.
findings.fix: Require email confirmation before counting or exposing guest RSVPs, and de-duplicate by verified identity.

controls.title: Guest RSVP can store someone else email/name without mailbox proof
controls.expected: Guest identity should not be trusted or exposed until email ownership is confirmed.

controls.title: Guest RSVP spam bypasses nominal limiter with varied headers
controls.expected: Public RSVP should use trusted-IP plus event/account abuse controls.

findings.title: Event attendance and profile history are visible by username
findings.cwe: CWE-359 Exposure of Private Personal Information
findings.rootCause: Authenticated users can query another user by username and receive event attendance or center group history.
findings.fix: Limit to self/admin or expose only explicitly public profile data through non-email identifiers.

controls.title: Any authenticated user can fetch another username event attendance via legacy POST
controls.expected: Event attendance history should be self/admin or intentionally public only.

controls.title: Any authenticated user can fetch another username event attendance via profile route
controls.expected: Profile event history should not be queryable by email-like username.

controls.title: Any authenticated user can fetch another username center group
controls.expected: Center membership should be exposed only through explicit public profile policy.

findings.title: Public event reads are unbounded and expose management identifiers
findings.rootCause: Legacy public event read paths default to full unpaginated responses and include createdBy IDs.
findings.fix: Require pagination, remove management identifiers from public payloads, and add cache-safe response shaping.

controls.title: fetchAllEvents defaults to unbounded public event dump
controls.expected: Public event listing should require bounded pagination by default.

sample.description: Annual leadership planning retreat. Requires verified center membership.

controls.title: Normal member can edit null-owner legacy event
controls.expected: Null-owner legacy events should be admin-locked or backfilled.

event.title: AE null owner edited by member

controls.title: Downgraded creator can still edit event
controls.expected: Current coordinator authorization should be required for edits.

controls.title: Downgraded creator can still view guest roster PII
controls.expected: Roster PII should require current coordinator/admin authority.

controls.title: Downgraded creator can still delete event
controls.expected: Delete should require current coordinator/admin authority.

controls.title: Authenticated RSVP succeeds for external-only event with allowJanataSignup=false
controls.expected: Server should reject native RSVP when event policy says external signup only.

controls.title: Guest RSVP succeeds for external-only event with allowJanataSignup=false
controls.expected: Guest API should enforce the same signup policy as the UI.

controls.title: Guest RSVP can store someone else email/name without mailbox proof
controls.expected: Guest identity should not be trusted or exposed until email ownership is confirmed.

controls.title: Guest RSVP spam bypasses nominal limiter with varied headers
controls.expected: Public RSVP should use trusted-IP plus event/account abuse controls.

controls.title: Any authenticated user can fetch another username event attendance via legacy POST
controls.expected: Event attendance history should be self/admin or intentionally public only.

controls.title: Any authenticated user can fetch another username event attendance via profile route
controls.expected: Profile event history should not be queryable by email-like username.

controls.title: Any authenticated user can fetch another username center group
controls.expected: Center membership should be exposed only through explicit public profile policy.

controls.title: fetchAllEvents defaults to unbounded public event dump
controls.expected: Public event listing should require bounded pagination by default.

sample.title: Members Only - Leadership Retreat
sample.description: Annual leadership planning retreat. Requires verified center membership.
sample.address: 10160 Clayton Rd, San Jose, CA - 95127, US

controls.title: fetchEventsByCenter POST defaults to unbounded public event dump
controls.expected: Center event listing should require bounded pagination by default.

sample.title: AE null owner edited by member

controls.title: Public event payload exposes createdBy management user id
controls.expected: Public event payload should not expose internal management identifiers unless needed.

controls.title: Registration state endpoint returns 200 for nonexistent event id
controls.expected: Registration endpoint should 404 nonexistent events to avoid misleading state.
